items.title: Rogers — Public Affairs
items.meta: $30K CAD · created May 11, advanced to Proposal May 22
items.rationale: Rogers is an existing Tier 1 telco partner. Public Affairs is a new internal team — same logo, new buyer.
items.coaching: Janessa on vacation — follow up May 27–28. Classic expansion play: new team, proven account.

items.title: Felix — Affordable Pricing Campaign
items.meta: $20K CAD · created May 21, advanced to Proposal May 22
items.rationale: Prior Felix campaign wrapped this week (3-contact thread). New budget cycle opened immediately. Sara Ross also booked a discovery call the same week.
items.coaching: Strong post-campaign expansion. Sara is the champion — keep her close.

items.title: Alan — CWHL Phase 2
items.meta: $15K CAD · created and reached Proposal May 22
items.rationale: Explicit Phase 2 of an existing CWHL partnership — Q&A placements, web articles, social video. Jacob Batist intro call held this week.
items.coaching: EOW noted ~$10K estimate; deal logged at $15K — good upsell. Phase continuations close faster than net-new.

items.title: Lums AI
items.meta: $4K CAD · created and reached Proposal May 20
items.rationale: New logo. Converted from warm intro (Marion Kesteloot). Only true net-new proposal this week.
items.coaching: Budget meeting June 2. Long sales cycle expected — flag if it stalls past June. Smallest deal of the week at 6% of pipeline.

items.title: Where we are
items.meta: Through May 25, 2026
items.rationale: 23 proposals since the plan started (~April 1). At 5/week, ~40 would be expected by now — cumulative pace is behind.

items.title: Required weekly rate
items.meta: 163 proposals remaining · 31 weeks left
items.rationale: Need 5.3 proposals/week from here to hit 186 by year-end. This week's 4 is 1.3 below the required rate.
items.coaching: The gap is manageable but compounding. Two strong weeks at 6+ proposals would put us back on track.

items.title: Sensitivity check
items.meta: If pace slips to 3/week
items.rationale: We'd land at ~116 by year-end — roughly $380K short of the $1M target.
items.coaching: Cold outreach is the lever. Zero cold emails this week means no new logos entering the top of the funnel.

items.title: Rogers — Public Affairs
items.meta: $30K · 43% of week's pipeline
items.rationale: Largest deal. Rogers is a proven buyer — above-average close-rate expectation.

items.title: Felix — Affordable Pricing Campaign
items.rationale: Strong follow-on size from a client that just finished a prior campaign.

items.title: Alan — CWHL Phase 2
items.rationale: Right at plan average ($17.5K). Well-scoped.

items.title: Lums AI
items.rationale: Below average for a first-time deal.
items.coaching: Net-new logos are trending small. Tier 1 cold prospecting (banks, insurers, telcos) would anchor larger first deals.

items.title: 7 inbound calendar bookings
items.meta: Abby Fong (TD), Flora Berwick (RBC), Sara Ross (Felix), Jacob Batist (Alan), Nikita Shivdasani (SP PR), Denise Chow, Catch-up
items.rationale: Self-booked via Michelle's calendar link. Abby Fong (TD) and Flora Berwick (RBC) are high-value — both tied to active deal threads.
items.coaching: Strong inbound conversion. EL and L+L programmes are driving people to self-book — worth tracking which programme drove each booking.

items.title: 3 existing account meetings
items.meta: The Peak / RBC (Jun 2), The Peak <> TD (May 26), The Peak <> Bell (May 28)
items.rationale: Bell AI Fabric ($50K) is in Proposal stage. TD is in active deal threads. RBC has three parallel tracks — Op-Ed, performance review, and agency-side opportunity.

items.title: 5 relationship / intro meetings
items.meta: Laura McEnery (Fintel/RBC), Jordan Lavin (Nutrilawn), Alena Atkinson (Burson), Olivia ×2
items.rationale: Alena at Burson is especially valuable — Burson is the most-touched prospect in the file with a $9K deal in Contract Sent.

items.title: 2 net-new discovery meetings
items.meta: Discovery Feels Human Again, The Peak <> FH
items.rationale: New contacts, no prior thread. Good signs of organic inbound.

items.title: TD — Smart Money (Auto + Business Banking)
items.meta: 9 emails across 2 contacts: Kiana Crummy (auto angle) + Abby Fong (business banking)
items.rationale: Most email volume on a single deal this week. Two separate TD contacts being worked simultaneously on different angles of the same Smart Money opportunity.
items.coaching: With 9 emails and Abby now booked for a discovery call, a proposal is likely within 1–2 weeks. Prepare the deck now.

items.title: Tangerine (via Rethink — Jake Kislinsky)
items.meta: Active pipeline follow-up
items.rationale: Deal has been in Proposal stage since May 1. Jake is the agency contact. EOW noted 'holding out hope' — needs a clear next step.

items.title: B&R RFP (via Media Futures Group — Simay Inan)
items.meta: RFP response thread
items.rationale: Formal RFP being worked — Media Futures Group acting as intermediary.

items.title: Alan — CWHL (Jacob Batist)
items.meta: Deal thread that led to Phase 2 proposal
items.rationale: Active deal conversation this week converted to Proposal stage. Good example of active deal work producing a proposal.

items.title: Fitzrovia / RBC (via BlueSky — Morgan Murphy)
items.meta: Agency-side RBC opportunity
items.rationale: Agency contact acting on behalf of an RBC team. Separate from the direct RBC relationship.

cold.title: Cold outreach — new account opening
cold.summary: 0 emails · below the 5/week floor · HubSpot check needed

items.title: Zero new-account emails logged this week
items.meta: All outbound was in existing threads (Re:) or warm follow-ups
items.rationale: No first-ever outreach to a brand-new contact was logged in HubSpot this week. All outbound emails were replies to prior conversations. If cold outreach happened via LinkedIn or other channels and wasn't logged in HubSpot, it won't appear here.
items.coaching: Cold outreach is the only way to open genuinely new accounts. The EL and L+L engine is excellent for warming up contacts, but it doesn't replace first-touch prospecting. Recommend blocking 30 min/day for cold reach from the bank comms list.

items.title: Priority sectors with zero touches this week
items.meta: MBA programs · Insurance comms · ETF providers (beyond Harvest) · Betakit/The Logic advertisers
items.rationale: Four of the named H2 target sectors had zero new outreach this week.
items.coaching: Pick 2 contacts per sector next week. Even 8 first-touch emails across these sectors would open 2–3 new conversations per month.

expansion.title: Expansion
expansion.summary: 18 emails · EL programme + new team intros + new products to existing clients

items.title: Emerging Leaders — Burson Global (Alena + Alex)
items.meta: 8 emails across 2 contacts · Alena Atkinson + Alex Benac
items.rationale: Burson is The Peak's most-touched account this week. Alena is deep in the EL conversation; Alex is on a separate EL thread. Both are being pitched The Peak's EL sponsorship package. Burson EL deal ($9K) is already in Contract Sent.
items.coaching: With a deal already signed and two contacts active, Burson is the clearest account to bring into a bigger package conversation. Adam Schwartz (Burson) is also on the Lunch + Learn track.

items.title: Emerging Leaders — Dialogue, #paid, NarrativeXPR, SP PR, Council of Canadian Innovators
items.meta: 5 contacts · 1 email each · EL hook outreach
items.rationale: These are existing contacts (prior intro calls or warm connections) being pitched the EL sponsorship package. Dialogue ($10K deal in Proposal) and SP PR (Nikita booked a discovery call) are furthest along.
items.coaching: EL outreach to existing warm accounts is the right expansion play. Track which of these convert to a proposal — that's the metric that matters.

items.title: Emerging Leaders — Google (Wendy Manton)
items.meta: 1 email · Big Tech sector
items.rationale: Google is one of the H2 priority Big Tech targets. Wendy is the contact being worked via EL outreach. She's also now scheduled for next week's meetings.
items.coaching: Google is a high-value target. Continue with the EL hook and book the intro call.

items.title: TD — New team intro (Ada Rampersaud)
items.meta: Dinner invite via Brooke intro · new team, not current deal contact
items.rationale: Ada is on a TD team that doesn't overlap with the current Smart Money deal. Brooke (existing contact) made the intro. This could open a parallel deal track within TD.

items.title: RBC — Peak Perspectives Op-Ed pitch (Habiba Ahmad)
items.meta: 4 emails · new product pitch to existing account
items.rationale: Habiba is an RBC contact introduced via warm referral. The Peak Perspectives Op-Ed is being pitched as a new product line — different from the campaign and performance tracks active with other RBC contacts.
items.coaching: RBC now has 3 parallel tracks running. Confirm there's no channel conflict between Habiba (Op-Ed), Flora (campaign performance), and the BlueSky/Simrin RBC agency track.

nurture_top_of_mind.title: Nurture: top of mind
nurture_top_of_mind.summary: 5 emails · past contacts staying warm with updates

items.title: Dan LeBaron — Zeno Group
items.meta: Media kit sent post-meeting
items.rationale: Post-intro call follow-up with a media kit. Dan is a warm contact being kept engaged ahead of a future pitch.
items.coaching: Zeno is a conversion candidate. Set a 2-week check-in to follow up on the media kit.

items.title: Ryan Sebastain — Deloitte Comms
items.meta: Meeting rescheduling thread
items.rationale: Deloitte comms team was referenced in Michelle's calendar as a next-week target. Active rescheduling suggests a warm relationship being maintained.

items.title: Emma Clarkin — NATIONAL PR
items.meta: Post-intro: media kit + L+L pitch sent
items.rationale: Emma had an intro call this week and immediately received a media kit. The L+L pitch is a fast-follow — NATIONAL PR is being treated as a high-priority agency target.

items.title: Nancy D'Souza — Weber Shandwick
items.meta: Post-L+L gift card delivery + congratulatory note
items.rationale: Weber Shandwick L+L was completed. Nancy is being thanked and kept warm. Referrals or a proposal conversation would be natural next steps.
items.coaching: Weber Shandwick completed their L+L — this is a prime moment to pitch a package. Don't let the warm moment cool off.

nurture_value.title: Nurture: value add
nurture_value.summary: 10 emails · L&L scheduling + Smart Money editorial opportunities

items.title: Lunch + Learn scheduling — Bensimon Byrne (Anya Lomako)
items.meta: 3 emails · locking the date
items.rationale: Bensimon Byrne L+L is being scheduled for May 28. Anya is coordinating food preferences and RSVP count. This is an agency relationship The Peak is investing in.
items.coaching: L+L is a high-value relationship tool when it leads to a proposal. Track the Bensimon Byrne conversion over the next 4 weeks.

items.title: Lunch + Learn scheduling — Category Comms (Meagan)
items.meta: 3 emails · June 10 confirmed
items.rationale: Category Comms reached out about a L+L after an intro call — inbound interest is a strong signal.

items.title: Lunch + Learn — Agnostic (Sarah / Nwhitten)
items.meta: 2 emails · gift card coordination
items.rationale: Agnostic L+L is complete. Gift card was arranged. Existing relationship being deepened.

items.title: Podcast partnership + L+L — Burson (Adam Schwartz)
items.meta: Podcast partnership chat done · L+L booked July 7
items.rationale: Burson is being pitched two value-add products simultaneously: a podcast partnership and a July L+L. Strongest multi-product pitch in the file.
items.coaching: Adam + Burson is the clearest candidate for a bundled package proposal.

items.title: L+L pitch — Zeno (Dan LeBaron)
items.meta: L+L mentioned as part of media kit pitch
items.rationale: Zeno was pitched the L+L as a relationship-building offer alongside the media kit.

items.title: Capital One — Smart Money interview offer (via OMC — Noah MacLean)
items.meta: 2 emails · no active deal in HubSpot
items.rationale: Capital One is being nurtured with a Smart Money executive interview offer — a value-add pitch rather than a direct sales conversation. No deal exists in HubSpot yet. This is the right way to open the account: lead with editorial value, convert to a proposal once they engage.
items.coaching: If Capital One engages on the Smart Money offer, that's the trigger to create a deal in HubSpot and move to active deal tracking.

items.title: Ada Rampersaud — TD (new team)
items.meta: Dinner/drinks invite via Brooke intro
items.rationale: Brooke (an existing TD contact) introduced Michelle to Ada, who is on a separate TD team. Michelle followed up with a drinks invite — turning a warm intro into a real relationship.
items.coaching: Ada is the most interesting new relationship this week. A dinner or coffee converts a referral into a real contact who can become a buyer.

items.title: Sasha Omerasevic — Bell
items.meta: Coffee rescheduled (Bell AI Fabric deal active)
items.rationale: Sasha is on the Bell team tied to the $50K AI Fabric deal. A coffee alongside the deal conversation strengthens the personal relationship that closes big deals.

items.title: Jenessa Crognali — Rogers (Public Affairs)
items.meta: Post-call follow-up while Janessa on vacation
items.rationale: Jenessa is the Rogers contact tied to the $30K Public Affairs proposal. Following up while she's on vacation is low-pressure, keeps the deal top of mind.

items.title: Alena Atkinson — Burson Global
items.meta: Ongoing relationship thread + meeting booked (Jun 16)
items.rationale: Alena is Michelle's deepest Burson relationship. A June 16 meeting was booked this week. With $9K already signed and a L+L + podcast pitch active, Alena is the internal champion to cultivate.

items.title: Iran Karimian — Google
items.meta: Following up on a cancelled client visit
items.rationale: Google visit was cancelled; Michelle followed up to reschedule. Persistence on a cancelled touch is good relationship behaviour.
